purpose: Score based on WHAT product was quoted, at WHAT price, and HOW the quotation process went.

features.reason: Specific product model — certain models have higher close rates due to popularity or fit
Chiller vs Cooler — category affects deal complexity and buyer type
centrifugal / screw / scroll / portable / floor_stand etc. — product type drives price tier and deal complexity
Complexity score (1–3) — complex products require more education; affects velocity and close rate
Numeric capacity of the product — higher capacity = bigger deal = longer cycle
Unit of capacity (ton, gallon, evap_cmh) — normalizes capacity across different product types
Bucketed capacity range — categorical grouping helps LGBM capture nonlinear price-capacity effects
Product model + capacity combined — composite key identifying unique product-capacity SKUs
Base price in AED — absolute price level signals deal size and buyer segment
VAT percentage — affects final deal cost; relevant for price sensitivity
Number of units ordered — bulk orders signal enterprise intent and higher commitment
Total before tax — direct measure of deal value
Tax computed on deal — relative tax load affects final decision
Final deal value — the actual amount the buyer needs to commit to
low / mid / high tier — categorical price classification captures buyer segment behavior patterns
Price per unit of capacity — efficiency metric; extreme ratios signal unusual deals
Cost of installation — free installation significantly lowers buyer friction
Whether installation is free — free installation is a strong conversion nudge
Whether a quotation was actually sent — one of the strongest direct pre-close indicators
Number of quote revisions — multiple revisions = active negotiation; lead is engaged but price-sensitive
Channel used to send quotation (EMAIL/WHATSAPP/BOTH) — channel of quote delivery affects response rate
Whether feedback was captured on the quotation — feedback presence confirms the lead reviewed the quote
Enterprise vs SMB flag — enterprise deals have different scoring dynamics (longer cycle, higher value)
Industry-level baseline close rate — anchors the product score against sector-specific conversion rates
Current pipeline stage — position in funnel is a structural signal for quotation relevance
Repeat customer flag — repeat customers accept quotes faster with fewer revisions
Days since lead entered pipeline — longer duration with quotation pending = higher risk of drop-off
Overall pipeline speed — fast-moving leads with a quotation are very close to closing
